parsed.summary: The diff implements two surgical, low-risk changes: (1) a UX improvement in the `commands/setup.md` skill command that adds an interactive `AskUserQuestion` menu when `/glm:setup` is run bare on an already-configured system, and (2) an un-scoping of the `package.` name from `@skylab/glm-plugin-cc` to `glm-plugin-cc`. Failure path trace: if the new menu is malformed or `AskUserQuestion` fails, the skill prompt simply errors, leaving the existing configuration untouched since the companion script itself is unchanged and all actions are user-initiated. Explicit flags bypass the menu entirely, preserving all automated/scripted workflows. The `package.` rename has zero functional impact on a `"private": true` package with no internal importers. Version parity is correctly maintained across all three JSON metadata files. No code, schema, or API surfaces are modified.
next_steps: Execute the 6 manual UAT scenarios (A-F) outlined in the release card to verify the interactive menu behaves correctly in a live Claude Code session.
Run `npm run ci:local` to confirm static checks, the 65 existing tests, and manifest validation all pass.
Run the planned `/codex:adversarial-review` on the full feature branch context.
